headline: GOLD RETREATS to $4,728 — 15% Below January's Record $5,595 High As Bull Market Pauses
snippet: Gold traded at $4,728/oz on April 13, up $1,517 year-over-year but roughly 15% below the January 2026 all-time high of $5,595. Analysts watch whether this pullback becomes a buying opportunity or signals a deeper correction.

headline: Americas Gold & Silver DISCOVERS 10 New High-Grade Veins at Galena — 4,896 g/t Ag Over 1.3m, Launches 64,000m Drill Campaign
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver (TSX: USA) has discovered 10 new high-grade silver-copper-antimony and silver-lead veins at its Galena Complex, including 4,896 g/t Ag and 3.95% Cu over 1.3m. The company is now launching its largest-ever drill program at approximately 64,000 metres across operations in 2026.

headline: G Mining Ventures ACQUIRES G2 Goldfields In $2.2B All-Share Deal — 500,000+ oz/yr District-Scale Complex In Guyana
snippet: G Mining Ventures agreed to acquire G2 Goldfields in a ~$2.2 billion all-share transaction, combining two adjacent assets in Guyana into a district-scale complex capable of producing more than 500,000 oz of gold annually — one of the largest junior mining deals of 2026.

headline: COPPER Hits Record $13,225/Tonne On LME — JPMorgan Projects 330,000-Tonne Refined Deficit For 2026
snippet: Benchmark LME copper reached $13,225/tonne, up over 30% year-on-year, as J.P. Morgan forecasts a 330,000-tonne refined copper shortfall this year. Tariff fears and AI-driven data centre demand are adding urgency to an already tight structural supply picture.

headline: Silver Heads For SIXTH Straight Annual Supply Deficit In 2026 — Silver Institute Projects 67M Oz Shortfall
snippet: The Silver Institute projects a 2026 supply deficit of ~67 million ounces, the sixth consecutive annual shortfall, even as mine output and recycling climb to a decade-high 1.05 billion ounces. China imported 790+ tonnes of silver in early 2026, the highest pace in eight years, tightening global supply further.

headline: Lithium Demand From Energy Storage To SURGE 55% In 2026 — Spodumene Prices Climbing Off Multi-Year Lows
snippet: Energy storage applications are expected to drive a 55% jump in lithium demand this year, lifting storage's share of total consumption from 23% to 31%. A recovery in spodumene prices since late 2025 has producers reversing shutdown decisions as the market sees its first sustained green shoots.

headline: RIO TINTO + GLENCORE Resume Merger Talks — $260B Deal Would Create World's Largest Mining Company
snippet: Rio Tinto and Glencore have resumed negotiations on a potential $260 billion merger that would forge the world's largest mining company, a so-called supermajor with dominant positions in copper, coal, and nickel. S&P Global analysis outlines what consolidation at this scale means for metals pricing and junior deal flow.

headline: Ex-Rio CEO Tom Albanese Takes SEABED MINING Public — American Ocean Minerals Merges With Odyssey In $1B Deal
snippet: American Ocean Minerals Corp., led by former Rio Tinto CEO Tom Albanese, is combining with Odyssey Marine Exploration in a ~$1 billion reverse merger, creating a publicly listed seabed mining vehicle. The deal marks a significant bet on deep-sea polymetallic nodule resources as a future critical metals supply source.

headline: URANIUM Breaks $101/Lb For First Time In 2 Years — U.S. DOE Pledges $2.7B For Domestic Enrichment Build-Out
snippet: Uranium spot prices surged 25% in January 2026 to $101.41/lb — the first time above $100 in two years — before pulling back on geopolitical shock. The U.S. Department of Energy followed with a $2.7 billion, 10-year commitment to expand domestic enrichment capacity, signalling long-term state support for the sector.

headline: TRUMP Launches $12B 'Project Vault' Critical Minerals Stockpile — Rare Earth Stocks JUMP On Announcement
snippet: The Trump administration launched Project Vault, a strategic minerals stockpile combining ~$2 billion in private capital with a $10 billion U.S. Export-Import Bank loan. Rare earth and critical minerals stocks surged on the announcement, which targets domestic supply security in 17 key minerals where China controls over 70% of refining.

headline: Critical Metals Greenlights Arctic Pilot Plant At Tanbreez — GREENLAND Rare Earth Project On Track For May 2026
snippet: Critical Metals Corp. has approved construction of a multi-use storage and pilot processing facility at Qaqortoq, Greenland, designed for Arctic conditions and slated for completion by May 2026. The Tanbreez project is among the largest known rare earth deposits outside China.

headline: GHANA Eyes 12% Sliding Gold Royalty — Industry Warns Investment Will Flee To Friendlier Jurisdictions
snippet: Ghana's government is proposing to replace its current 3–5% fixed gold royalty with a sliding scale reaching 12%, a move that would more than double costs for producers at current prices. Major mining companies operating in the country have warned the plan would deter new exploration investment and accelerate capital flight.

headline: Gold MINERS Lagged The Metal By 20-25% In 2025 — Historical Patterns Point To A Spring Catch-Up Rally
snippet: While spot gold surged 60%+ in 2025, the GDX senior miners ETF gained only 35–40% and junior miners lagged further still. Analysis of the past 25 years of gold bull markets shows miners historically outperform the metal dramatically from mid-March through early June — a window now open.
